title: Dividends
description: How to report dividends when you lodge your return using myTax.

content_blocks.text: This section is about dividends from shares in Australian companies.
We have pre-filled your tax return with dividend information provided to us. Check for dividends you received that are not shown in the information that has been pre-filled and add them.

items: dividends applied under a dividend reinvestment plan
dividends that were dealt with on your behalf
bonus shares that qualify as dividends
distributions by a corporate limited partnership
dividends paid by a corporate unit trust, public trading trust or listed investment company.

content_blocks.text: Earnings you received, or were credited with, on a non-share equity interest may also be included as dividends.
You will need your statements from each Australian company that paid you dividends or made distributions to you between 1 July 2014 and 30 June 2015 inclusive.
Do not include dividends or a distribution on which family trust distribution tax has been paid.
Go to Dividends and franking credits to find out how to complete this question if:

items: you received a dividend as a result of a dividend washing arrangement
you had shares and were under an obligation to make, or were likely to make, a related payment
you, within 45 days of buying shares (90 days for certain preference shares), either sold them, or entered into an arrangement to reduce the risk of making a loss on them.

content_blocks.text: Go to Amounts that are treated as unfranked dividends to find out how to complete this question if you received or were credited with amounts:

items: by a private company, as a shareholder or associate of a shareholder, in the form of payments, loans or debts forgiven

by a trustee of a trust in the form of payments, loans or debts forgiven where a private company in which you were a shareholder, or an associate of a shareholder, had an unpaid present entitlement from the trust.

Look at your information and check any details pre-filled in your tax return. Add missing dividends by clicking on the 'Add dividend' button and completing all the information. Include details of any joint accounts.

content_text:
This section is about dividends from shares in Australian companies.
We have pre-filled your tax return with dividend information provided to us. Check for dividends you received that are not shown in the information that has been pre-filled and add them.
Dividends and distributions include:
- dividends applied under a dividend reinvestment plan
- dividends that were dealt with on your behalf
- bonus shares that qualify as dividends
- distributions by a corporate limited partnership
- dividends paid by a corporate unit trust, public trading trust or listed investment company.
Earnings you received, or were credited with, on a non-share equity interest may also be included as dividends.
## Answering this question
You will need your statements from each Australian company that paid you dividends or made distributions to you between 1 July 2014 and 30 June 2015 inclusive.
Do not include dividends or a distribution on which family trust distribution tax has been paid.
Find out more
Go to Dividends and franking credits to find out how to complete this question if:
- you received a dividend as a result of a dividend washing arrangement
- you had shares and were under an obligation to make, or were likely to make, a related payment
- you, within 45 days of buying shares (90 days for certain preference shares), either sold them, or entered into an arrangement to reduce the risk of making a loss on them.
Go to Amounts that are treated as unfranked dividends to find out how to complete this question if you received or were credited with amounts:
- by a private company, as a shareholder or associate of a shareholder, in the form of payments, loans or debts forgiven
- by a trustee of a trust in the form of payments, loans or debts forgiven where a private company in which you were a shareholder, or an associate of a shareholder, had an unpaid present entitlement from the trust.
## Completing your tax return
- Look at your information and check any details pre-filled in your tax return. Add missing dividends by clicking on the 'Add dividend' button and completing all the information. Include details of any joint accounts.
- Click on 'Save'.
